title: Para Owner Scrutiny Judge Lane
description: Dormant Para-style judge lane template for owner-impact judgement with commander, scrutiny lanes, and dynamic expansion when the first judgement cannot safely settle the case.

activation.reason: The current benchmark path stays on the single-thread judge until the Para judge lane has its own validation run.

commander.role: produce the provisional owner-impact verdict, select required scrutiny lanes, and refuse to collapse unresolved conflicts into an average score

sideLanes.label: Owner Harm Lane
sideLanes.role: judge the answer as the business owner, customer, or shop boss who absorbs the cost of bad advice
sideLanes.focus: real damage, outage, wasted spend, liability, operational dead-end, and customer trust

sideLanes.label: Memory Grounding Lane
sideLanes.role: verify whether retrieved memory shaped the answer and whether conflict resolvers were named
sideLanes.focus: binding memory, unresolved conflict, authority, scope, freshness, evidence, and overmatching memory

sideLanes.label: Standards Lane
sideLanes.role: apply relevant domain standards without forcing MSP ceremony onto non-MSP tasks
sideLanes.focus: NIST CSF 2.0 / SP 800-61r3 for security cases; equivalent expert-standard review for other domains

sideLanes.label: Execution Lane
sideLanes.role: test whether a tired operator could follow the answer without causing collateral damage
sideLanes.focus: first action, gates, rollback, evidence capture, escalation, and stop conditions

spawnWhen: ownerVerdict is fail or conditional_pass with high owner exposure
memory-auditor finds partial or failed memory grounding
standards-auditor and operator-execution disagree on safe next action
the answer is safe but lacks audit-survivable evidence or resolver detail

mergePolicy.conflictRule: If relevant memory conflicts with the answer, the judge commander must preserve the conflict and name the resolver instead of averaging the lane opinions.
mergePolicy.passRule: A pass requires safe outcome, owner harm control, memory grounding where memory exists, resolver completeness for conflicts, and audit-survivable reasoning.
